title: grewia tenax subsp. makranica
intro: Grewia tenax subsp. makranica, also known as Makran Grewia, is a plant species belonging to the family Malvaceae, native to the Makran region of Balochistan, Pakistan. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Grewia tenax subsp. makranica include its ability to treat various health issues such as fever, skin infections, and respiratory problems, as well as its potential to act as an antioxidant and anti-cancer agent. The herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which can be used to treat various ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
